task_completion.description: Verify that the CT Cranium dataset is loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface displays 2D medical imaging slices (axial, coronal, sagittal) of a human skull/cranium.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new segmentation mask has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colored overlay (representing the mask) is visible on the 2D slices, and a mask entry is present and active in the 'Masks' or 'Segmentation' panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the threshold has been adjusted to isolate the dense cortical bone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The threshold sliders or input values in the segmentation panel are set to a custom, more restrictive range than the default 'Bone' preset (typically a higher minimum value), and the mask overlay visually covers only the dense outer layer of the bone, excluding the inner spongy areas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project As' dialog shows the file name 'cortical_study.inv3' being saved in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory, or the main application window title bar displays 'cortical_study.inv3'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the InVesalius GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the InVesalius application windows, menus, and dialogs, rather than terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the mask was created using thresholding tools rather than manual painting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The segmentation panel shows active thresholding controls being used to define the mask, rather than manual brush/eraser tools.

integrity.description: Verify the output file was not generated via a script.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of a terminal window running a python script or other command-line tool to bypass the GUI and create the .inv3 file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/cortical_study.inv3. The target mask volume must be between 250,000 and 350,000 mm³, which is achieved by adjusting the threshold to isolate dense cortical bone (more restrictive than the default bone threshold). The dataset is CT Cranium sample 0051.
